title: SpinalExpect_NonInvasive
description: This dataset comprises non-invasive spinal cord recordings from 30 healthy young subjects performing an auditory-somatosensory expectation task. Participants received electric median nerve stimulations preceded by auditory cues with either fixed (1100 ms) or variable (100-2100 ms) inter-stimulus intervals across alternating blocks. The study investigates whether temporal expectation modulates initial sensory processing in the human spinal cord, with recordings obtained using a novel high-frequency electrode montage positioned in three rings around the cervical spine.
methods_description: Non-invasive spinal cord recordings were obtained using electrodes arranged in three rings around the neck at the level of the sixth cervical vertebra. Subjects received electric median nerve stimulations on the left wrist preceded by auditory cues. The inter-stimulus interval was either constant (1100 ms) or randomly varied (100-2100 ms uniform distribution) across alternating blocks. Rare trials contained two median nerve stimuli in rapid succession (80 ms inter-stimulus interval). Most subjects completed two days of testing.
